Petition For Appointment Of Successor Trustee
Off Calendar Jun-02-2026 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Mercy Hall appeared via zoom for and with Margaret Pinkard (petitioner).
Amy Harrington for Cinda B. Montoya as conservatee (via Zoom).
Joshua Castillo appeared via zoom (grandson).

Ms. Harrington reports Ms. Montoya (conservatee)'s current status at Ivy Park in response to the Court's inquiry.

The Court confirms there is no objection to the Court's full supervision of the trust.

The Court orders a drop-off ex parte petition to remove the confidential document publicly filed in this case on August 22, 2025 and April 27, 2026. Ms. Hall acknowledges the issue and responds that necessary steps are undertaken to rectify the April 27, 2026 filing caused by her office. Ms. Hall also reports that she communicated the issue to the former counsel who caused the August 22, 2025 filing.

The Court indicates its intent to delay discharging the former trustees, pointing out that their final account must be settled before the Court can discharge them and exonerate their bond.

The Court points out that Ms. Pinckard's $5,801,000 bond as temporary successor trustee previously ordered has not been filed. The Court questions whether Ms. Pinckard has been administering the trust without being bonded. Ms. Hall responds that there was confusion due to her client's dual status as conservator. The Court confirms that Ms. Pinckard has given her bond as conservator as ordered in the related conservatorship case.

The Court indicates its intent to fix Ms. Pinckard's bond as successor trustee at $174,000 based on the annual income from the real properties. The Court advises counsel that the value of the real properties is not bondable for a court-supervised trust. Ms. Harrington requests recording an instrument similar to letters of conservatorship to provide constructive notice that the trustee is not authorized to sell any real property without prior court approval. Ms. Hall reports that she previously recorded a certified copy of the order appointing her client as temporary successor trustee. Ms. Hall indicates her intent to do the same by recording a certified copy of the order on this petition.

Joshua Castillo inquires about Ms. Montoya's property in Mexico. Ms. Harrington responds that only real property located in California is an asset of this conservatorship, but the estimated value and location of the Mexican property, etc. will be provided to the Court by the conservator with her accounting.

The Court takes the petition under submission with its intent to grant.

Judge: Russell S. Roeca. Clerk: A. Datt. Not reported.

EX PARTE PETITION FOR APPOINTMENT OF TEMPORARY SUCCESSOR TRUSTEE

Petition To Remove Trustee, Bring Trust Under Court Supervision, And Appoint Successor Co-Trustees; And Petition For Fees On Account To Successor Co-Trustees
Off Calendar Jan-07-2026 Continued To Mar-10-26 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances:
Robert Little for temporary co-trustees Meredith Taylor and Rebecca Owen.
Rebecca Owen, temporary co-trustee.
Amy Harrington for Cinda B. Montoya as conservatee (via Zoom).
Barbara Castillo, daughter of Cinda B. Montoya (via Zoom).

The matter was called for hearing on Petition to Remove Trustee, Bring Trust Under Court Supervision, Appoint Successor Co-Trustees, and for Fees on Account to Successor Co-Trustees.

Mr. Little reported that a related petition to appoint a temporary conservator has been filed and is set for hearing. He further reported that a petition seeking appointment of a successor trustee is being prepared and requested that the existing order suspending trustees and placing the trust under court supervision remain in effect pending resolution of the related proceedings.

The Court addressed the status of a previously requested appointment of a probate referee and directed that courtesy copies of the request and proposed order be delivered to the Court.

Ms. Harrington stated her intent to continue participating in the trust matter and noted that an order expanding the scope of her authority has already been entered in the related conservatorship case.

The Court continued the matter to March 10, 2026, at 9:00 a.m. in Department 204, to allow time for the filing and hearing of the related petitions and for any outstanding issues to be addressed.

Judge: Russell S. Roeca; Clerk: Kenneth B. Hunt; Not reported (D204)

Petition To Remove Trustee, Bring Trust Under Court Supervision, And Appoint Successor Co-Trustees; And Petition For Fees On Account To Successor Co-T
Off Calendar Oct-29-2025 Continued To Jan-07-26 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances: Robert Little appeared via zoom for Meredith Taylor and Rebecca Owen (temporary Co-trustee's).
Andrew Shultz appeared via zoom for Peggy Rendon (petitioner).
Amy Harrington appeared via zoom for Cinda Montoya (conservatee).

Mr. Shutlz expressed to the court that Ms. Rendon shall represent herself, that she is no longer the successor trustee, and that she in longer a party to this matter.

The Court granted the extension of the order that was made on September 2, 2025 at the ex parte hearing. Meredith Taylor and Rebecca Owen are to remain Temporary Successor Co-Trustees until January 7, 2026.

Discussion was held regarding the 8 parcels of land, that 5 real properties with a value of $5,000,000.00, that the estimated annual gross rental income is around $195,000.00.

The Petition is continued to January 7, 2026 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204.

Judge: Russell S. Roeca. Clerk: A. Datt. Deputy: Deleon. Not reported.
